tender.title: P0980 - Afghan Refugee Resettlement Schemes: Provision of orientation, accommodation and support services for Year 1  - AWARD
tender.description:
The Council wishes to establish a contract for the provision of Orientation, Accommodation and Support Services for the first 12 months following arrival, for 190 Afghan refugees arriving under the combined Afghan Citizens Resettlement Scheme (ACRS) and Afghan Relocations and Assistance Policy (ARAP). This scheme has been agreed between the Council and the Government.
       
      The Scheme requirements include the following:
       
      o Provision of accommodation and follow on welfare and tenancy support
      o Health care - including access to Primary and Secondary care including mental health services
      o Education and English language learning for both adults and children
      o Access to Social care 
      o Employment and training
      o Wellbeing care - health and safety information, emergency contacts clothing, food, including integration into local communities
      o General orientation - Promoting independence
      o Community integration
      
      The Contract will last for 3 years (unless terminated under the terms and conditions of contract).
      
      The contract will be awarded to a single provider.
      
      The Council will be using its free to use e-tendering system (in-tend) for the administration of this procurement process and potential suppliers must register with the system to be able to express an interest. If you wish to express an interest in this opportunity please click on the following link to access https://in-tendhost.co.uk/birminghamcc/ and submit your details to register. You will then be sent details on how to log on which will enable you to download all relevant Invitation to Tender (ITT) documentation.  
      
      Please note TUPE will apply to this contract. The TUPE information will be released to you upon receipt of a signed copy of Appendix - TUPE Confidentiality Agreement.  You are advised to fill this in as soon as possible on receipt and return via https://in-tendhost.co.uk/birminghamcc
      
      If you are unable to register with In-tend or have any questions or problems on how to use this web site please either email us at: cps@birmingham.gov.uk 
      
      Your completed tender submission should be returned by noon on 20th September 2022 via the 'in-tend' system https://in-tendhost.co.uk/birminghamcc

tender.title: Subleasing of shared housing for complex needs residents
tender.description:
The Council currently leases 23 HMO's from private landlords. This
project proposes that around 6 HMO's (equating to approx. 25-30 rooms)
are subleased to a specialist supported housing provider to facilitate the
provision of an enhanced supported housing package targeted
specifically at residents such as rough sleepers who are suffering from
mental health conditions and/or drug or alcohol dependency issues.
One of the main advantages of this proposal are that we can separate out
those clients who are solely single homeless people from those who have
more complex health and welfare needs in addition to their housing need.

tender.title: Social Housing Decarbonisation Fund (SHDF) wave 1
tender.description:
Delivery of wave 1 social housing retro-fit progamme - PAS2035 process and retrofit of energy efficiency and zero-carbon heating measures

The appointment of three suitably qualified installers (as a minimum with PAS2030:2019 Trustmark accreditation and qualifying material systems) and professional services providers (as a minimum with PAS2035 Assessor and Design accreditation) to deliver retrofit of insulation measures in 109 social homes where FHDC are the landlord in and around the towns in the district. 

The measures are funded by the wave 1 social housing decarbonisation fund (SHDF) and by FHDC funds

Direct Award via Fusion 21 Decarbonisation framework
Lot 1 Whole House Decarbonisation - Vinci Construction UK Limited & United Living Ltd
Lot 1 Energy Efficiency Framework - Insulation Limited

tender.title: Exploration of affordable housing for the SNEE nursing workforce
tender.description:
This is a Contract Award Notice to communicate a contract award by NHS Suffolk and North East Essex Integrated Care Board (the "ICB"). 

The contract award is made to Iceni Projects Limited for the provision of a data collection exercise, needs assessment, completion of a prospective report, and completion of an impact considerations document pertaining to affordable housing for the Suffolk and North East Essex nursing workforce.  

The overarching ambition is to support NHS staff, and in this instance, the nursing workforce, to be able to access housing that meets their needs in terms of affordability, demographics, and location to their place of work. 

This contract commences on 14th November 2022 until 13th February 2023, at a total contract value of £29,700 (inclusive of VAT).   

The ICB is publishing this Contract Award Notice in the Contracts Finder in accordance with the Public Contracts Regulations 2015 (as amended by The Public Procurement (Amendment etc.) (EU Exit) Regulations 2020) for spend above £25,000.
